perturbation.original_text: Unless otherwise provided herein, all obligations under this Agreement shall terminate and cease to have any force or effect on the earlier of: (i) two years from the date hereof, or (ii) the date of any consummation of the Potential Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless otherwise provided herein, all obligations under this Agreement shall terminate and cease to have any force or effect on the earlier of: (i) one year from the date hereof, or (ii) the date of any consummation of the Potential Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by shortening the default term of the agreement from two years to one year. This directly conflicts with section 6, which refers to an 18-month period related to employee solicitation, thus creating uncertainty and enforceability issues regarding the actual duration of the agreement's obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Further, during the term of eighteen (18) months beginning on the date hereof, the Company shall not knowingly solicit or recruit management or executive level employees of ABRH who became known to the Company through work on the above transaction or purpose contemplated above, without the prior written consent of ABRH.
perturbation.justification: The changed text shortens the agreement's termination date from two years to one year. The contradicted text explicitly states an obligation lasting eighteen (18) months. Since the agreement now terminates after only one year (12 months), this directly contradicts the 18-month obligation, making the latter unenforceable for the full stated duration. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines or obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The Company expressly agrees that it will keep ABRH’s Confidential Information confidential and that neither the Company nor any of its affiliates nor any of its or their directors, officers, employees, operating partners, consultants, or advisors (including without limitation, attorneys and accountants) (collectively, but only to the extent that such persons actually receive Confidential Information, “Representatives”) will use, for its own benefit (other than in connection with the Potential Transaction), or communicate or disclose (orally, in writing, or in any other manner) to any other person or entity any Confidential Information, or that the Company or any of its Representatives have received or otherwise been provided access to Confidential Information by any other party.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company expressly agrees that it will keep ABRH’s Confidential Information confidential, except as required to comply with routine internal audits, and that neither the Company nor any of its affiliates nor any of its or their directors, officers, employees, operating partners, consultants, or advisors (including without limitation, attorneys and accountants) (collectively, but only to the extent that such persons actually receive Confidential Information, “Representatives”) will use, for its own benefit (other than in connection with the Potential Transaction), or communicate or disclose (orally, in writing, or in any other manner) to any other person or entity any Confidential Information, or that the Company or any of its Representatives have received or otherwise been provided access to Confidential Information by any other party.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds an exception to the confidentiality obligation, allowing disclosure for 'routine internal audits.' This directly contradicts the broad non-disclosure obligation outlined in the same section, creating uncertainty regarding what constitutes a 'routine' audit and which confidential information can be disclosed, thereby weakening the confidentiality protection and conflicting with the intentions specified in the original text regarding confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company expressly agrees that it will keep ABRH’s Confidential Information confidential, except as required to comply with routine internal audits, and that neither the Company nor any of its affiliates nor any of its or their directors, officers, employees, operating partners, consultants, or advisors (including without limitation, attorneys and accountants) (collectively, but only to the extent that such persons actually receive Confidential Information, “Representatives”) will use, for its own benefit (other than in connection with the Potential Transaction), or communicate or disclose (orally, in writing, or in any other manner) to any other person or entity any Confidential Information, or that the Company or any of its Representatives have received or otherwise been provided access to Confidential Information by any other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception ('except as required to comply with routine internal audits') to the confidentiality obligation. The contradicted text, which is actually identical to the changed text, now contains this exception, creating a direct contradiction within the document itself. The original text did not have this exception. This makes the obligation unclear and introduces a contradiction, because some confidentiality is now allowed.

perturbation.original_text: The Company will only use ABRH’s Confidential Information for the transaction contemplated above and will only disclose ABRH’s Confidential Information to its Representatives who need to know such information and who are informed of the terms of this Agreement and are directed to keep such Confidential Information confidential.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company will only use ABRH’s Confidential Information for the transaction contemplated above and may disclose ABRH’s Confidential Information to its Representatives. The Company is not responsible for monitoring the use of confidential information by its Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the 'need to know' restriction on disclosing confidential information to Representatives and removes the responsibility of the Company to monitor said confidential information. This contradicts the earlier clause where Representatives had to be informed of this agreement, and keep the confidential information secret. This also introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the level of control and responsibility the Company has over its Representatives' handling of ABRH's Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company expressly agrees that it will keep ABRH’s Confidential Information confidential, except as required to comply with routine internal audits, and that neither the Company nor any of its affiliates nor any of its or their directors, officers, employees, operating partners, consultants, or advisors (including without limitation, attorneys and accountants) (collectively, but only to the extent that such persons actually receive Confidential Information, “Representatives”) will use, for its own benefit (other than in connection with the Potential Transaction), or communicate or disclose (orally, in writing, or in any other manner) to any other person or entity any Confidential Information, or that the Company or any of its Representatives have received or otherwise been provided access to Confidential Information by any other party. The Company further agrees that it and its Representatives will not in any way cause or encourage another person to disclose ABRH’s Confidential Information to any person or entity or judicial or administrative agency or body for any reason whatsoever unless required to do so pursuant to legal process. The Company will only use ABRH’s Confidential Information for the transaction contemplated above and may disclose ABRH’s Confidential Information to its Representatives. The Company is not responsible for monitoring the use of confidential information by its Representatives. The Company will not, without ABRH’s written consent, disclose or issue any news release, announcement, denial, or confirmation of this Agreement or any actual or potential business negotiation or relationship between the parties. The Company and its Representatives receiving Confidential Information from ABRH will protect ABRH’s Confidential Information from both unauthorized use and disclosure by exercising at least the same degree of care that is used for similar information of its own, but no less than reasonable care.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the 'need to know' restriction and monitoring responsibility, directly contradicting the contradicted text which states that the Company must keep ABRH's Confidential Information confidential and that Representatives who receive Confidential Information must protect it from unauthorized use and disclosure. The original text implies a stricter control over the use of confidential information by its representatives. The changed text removes this control leading to an inconsistency regarding the responsibility of the Company over its representatives.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Company may retain such copies of the Confidential Information that reside on the Company’s back-up storage or archiving system, solely for document retention purposes and not for any other use, and for compliance purposes as required by law or to evidence compliance of Company’s obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Company may retain such copies of the Confidential Information that reside on the Company’s back-up storage or archiving system, solely for document retention purposes and for other internal use, and for compliance purposes as required by law or to evidence compliance of Company’s obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change broadens the permitted uses of retained Confidential Information from solely 'document retention' to include 'other internal use'. This contradicts the earlier non-disclosure agreement in section 3, creating an in-text contradiction and ambiguity as to what constitutes acceptable 'internal use' and potentially undermining the confidentiality protections afforded to ABRH's information. It weakens the restriction specified previously and allows for broad interpretation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company expressly agrees that it will keep ABRH’s Confidential Information confidential, except as required to comply with routine internal audits, and that neither the Company nor any of its affiliates nor any of its or their directors, officers, employees, operating partners, consultants, or advisors (including without limitation, attorneys and accountants) (collectively, but only to the extent that such persons actually receive Confidential Information, “Representatives”) will use, for its own benefit (other than in connection with the Potential Transaction), or communicate or disclose (orally, in writing, or in any other manner) to any other person or entity any Confidential Information, or that the Company or any of its Representatives have received or otherwise been provided access to Confidential Information by any other party. The Company further agrees that it and its Representatives will not in any way cause or encourage another person to disclose ABRH’s Confidential Information to any person or entity or judicial or administrative agency or body for any reason whatsoever unless required to do so pursuant to legal process. The Company will only use ABRH’s Confidential Information for the transaction contemplated above and may disclose ABRH’s Confidential Information to its Representatives. The Company is not responsible for monitoring the use of confidential information by its Representatives. The Company will not, without ABRH’s written consent, disclose or issue any news release, announcement, denial, or confirmation of this Agreement or any actual or potential business negotiation or relationship between the parties. The Company and its Representatives receiving Confidential Information from ABRH will protect ABRH’s Confidential Information from both unauthorized use and disclosure by exercising at least the same degree of care that is used for similar information of its own, but no less than reasonable care.
perturbation.justification: The changed text expands the permissible use of retained Confidential Information to include 'other internal use' in addition to document retention. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states the Company will only use ABRH’s Confidential Information for the transaction contemplated above and limits disclosure. The introduction of 'other internal use' creates ambiguity and conflicts with the explicit limitations on use and disclosure found in the contradicted text. Thus a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Regardless of the above, this provision shall not restrict the right of the Company to solicit or recruit such employees as a result of any substantial asset purchase or sale or merger or through the general use of the media, and the Company shall not be prohibited from hiring such employees who answer any general advertisement or otherwise voluntarily apply for hire without having been personally solicited or recruited by the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Regardless of the above, this provision shall not restrict the right of the Company to solicit or recruit such employees as a result of any substantial asset purchase or sale or merger or through the general use of the media. The Company shall not be prohibited from hiring such employees who answer any general advertisement or otherwise voluntarily apply for hire without proof of personal solicitation or recruitment by the Company.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause provided a clear exception: the company could hire employees who responded to general advertisements. This change, by adding 'without proof of personal solicitation or recruitment by the Company', creates an in-text contradiction by placing the burden of proof on whether solicitation occurred. This shift directly undermines the original exception, as now the company must actively prove they did not solicit the employee, rather than simply being allowed to hire those who voluntarily apply. It creates doubt and liability where none existed before and it makes that part of the clause harder to enforce.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company and its Representatives will make no contact, written or verbal, with any of ABRH’s management, staff, or employees not directly involved with the transaction contemplated above for purposes of evaluating the 400 West 48th Avenue Denver CO 80216 303.296.2121 Potential Transaction unless with ABRH’s written permission. Further, during the term of eighteen (18) months beginning on the date hereof, the Company shall not knowingly solicit or recruit management or executive level employees of ABRH who became known to the Company through work on the above transaction or purpose contemplated above, without the prior written consent of ABRH. Regardless of the above, this provision shall not restrict the right of the Company to solicit or recruit such employees as a result of any substantial asset purchase or sale or merger or through the general use of the media. The Company shall not be prohibited from hiring such employees who answer any general advertisement or otherwise voluntarily apply for hire without proof of personal solicitation or recruitment by the Company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the exception to the non-solicitation clause. The original text allowed hiring employees who responded to general ads without restriction. The changed text adds 'without proof of personal solicitation or recruitment by the Company,' which contradicts the intent of the exception. This creates a contradiction because the company now has the burden of proving they didn't solicit the employee, making it inconsistent with the original clause that allowed hiring based on general advertisement responses without needing to prove anything. This is exactly the kind of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' defined in the Perturbation Definitions.

perturbation.original_text: The parties irrevocably consent to the jurisdiction of the courts of the State of Colorado and of any federal court located within the State of Colorado for all purposes in connection with any action or proceeding that is brought by ABRH that arises out of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties irrevocably consent to the jurisdiction of the courts of the State of Colorado for all purposes in connection with any action or proceeding that is brought by ABRH that arises out of this Agreement. The Company reserves the right to pursue arbitration in lieu of litigation.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds a clause that contradicts the agreement to Colorado jurisdiction. The addition of 'The Company reserves the right to pursue arbitration in lieu of litigation' creates a contradiction. The first sentence states irrevocable consent to Colorado courts, while the second allows the Company to opt for arbitration, bypassing the agreed-upon jurisdiction. This introduces ambiguity and undermines the jurisdictional agreement, creating uncertainty about the proper forum for dispute resolution, thus, the parties are no longer fully consenting to Colorado courts.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Governing Law . This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Colorado without regard to its conflict of laws principles. The parties irrevocably consent to the jurisdiction of the courts of the State of Colorado for all purposes in connection with any action or proceeding that is brought by ABRH that arises out of this Agreement. The Company reserves the right to pursue arbitration in lieu of litigation.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the parties irrevocably consent to the jurisdiction of the courts of Colorado. The changed text adds a sentence that 'The Company reserves the right to pursue arbitration in lieu of litigation.' This creates an inconsistency as arbitration is an alternative to court jurisdiction. The contradicted text is exactly the same as the changed text, and the Perturbation Explanation confirms this creates a contradiction. Therefore, the changed text introduces an in-text contradiction.
